Genotyping QC pre-Imputation

Darren Cameron

2025-08-28

Genotyping pre-imputation QC steps

Steps 3-7 were carried out using the GenotypeQCtoHRC package. See the eqtlgenotypeshg38pretm.qc_report.html for those metrics. Some additional details for some of those tests are below and in the reports/03GENOTYPES-PREdirectory. The citation for GenotypeQCtoHRC is Lynham et al. 2023 (DOI:10.1192/bjo.2022.636).

  1. Rm duplicate sample (14493 and 144FC same sample. 14493 removed)
  2. Check autosomal heterozygosity
  3. Missingness:
  • Sample rm if > 5% markers missing,
  • SNPs excluded if missing in > 5% samples
  1. HWE p < 1 x 10-6
  2. Samples ambig. sex assignments removed (all passed)
  3. Relatedness assessed (duplicate sample identified 14493 and removed)
  4. Ancestry assessed
  5. Liftover hg19 to hg38
  6. Conversion of markers to dbSNP 156 (check this) using 1000G Phase 3
  7. Genotype Harmonizer used to align curated markers to the Trans-Omics for Precision Medicine (TopMED) imputation panel
  8. Remove ambiguous A/T, G/C SNP with MAF > 0.4 (none rm here does GenotypeQCtoHRC do this?),
  9. Remove SNPs MAF < 0.01

QC tables and plots

QC 2: Autosomal Heterozygosity

Tables

outliers_tbl <- het_tbl %>%
  filter(outlier_het | outlier_F) %>%
  select(FID, IID, outlier_het, outlier_F)
datatable(outliers_tbl, caption = 'Table 2. Autosomal heterozygosity oultliers table')
write_tsv(outliers_tbl, paste0(in_dir, "het_outliers.txt"), col_names = FALSE)

Plot

het_plot <- ggplot(het_tbl, aes(x = het_rate)) +
  geom_histogram(binwidth = 0.005, fill = "#4C78A8", color = "black", alpha = 0.7) +
  geom_vline(aes(xintercept = mean_het + 3 * sd_het), color = "#E31A1C", linetype = "dashed", size = 1) +
  geom_vline(aes(xintercept = mean_het - 3 * sd_het), color = "#E31A1C", linetype = "dashed", size = 1) +
  labs(
    title = "Heterozygosity Rate Distribution",
    x = "Heterozygosity Rate",
    y = "Count",
    caption = "Red dashed lines indicate ±3 SD thresholds"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 14, hjust = 0.5),
    axis.title = element_text(face = "bold"),
    axis.text = element_text(color = "black"),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    panel.border = element_rect(color = "black", fill = NA, size = 0.5),
    plot.caption = element_text(size = 10, hjust = 0)
  )
## Warning: Using `size` aesthetic for lines was deprecated in ggplot2 3.4.0.
## ℹ Please use `linewidth` instead.
## This warning is displayed once every 8 hours.
## Call `lifecycle::last_lifecycle_warnings()` to see where this warning was generated.
## Warning: The `size` argument of `element_rect()` is deprecated as of ggplot2 3.4.0.
## ℹ Please use the `linewidth` argument instead.
## This warning is displayed once every 8 hours.
## Call `lifecycle::last_lifecycle_warnings()` to see where this warning was generated.
# Create publication-ready F distribution plot
f_plot <- ggplot(het_tbl, aes(x = F)) +
  geom_histogram(binwidth = 0.01, fill = "#F28E2B", color = "black", alpha = 0.7) +
  geom_vline(aes(xintercept = mean_F + 3 * sd_F), color = "#E31A1C", linetype = "dashed", size = 1) +
  geom_vline(aes(xintercept = mean_F - 3 * sd_F), color = "#E31A1C", linetype = "dashed", size = 1) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = c(-0.15, 0.15), color = "#E31A1C", linetype = "solid", size = 1) +
  labs(
    title = "Inbreeding Coefficient (F) Distribution",
    x = "Inbreeding Coefficient (F)",
    y = "Count",
    caption = "Red lines: solid = [-0.15, 0.15], dashed = ±3 SD"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 14, hjust = 0.5),
    axis.title = element_text(face = "bold"),
    axis.text = element_text(color = "black"),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    panel.border = element_rect(color = "black", fill = NA, size = 0.5),
    plot.caption = element_text(size = 10, hjust = 0)
  )

combined_plot <- plot_grid(het_plot, f_plot, labels = c("A", "B"),
                           label_size = 14, label_fontface = "bold",
                           ncol = 2, align = "h")
combined_plot

#ggsave(paste0(ind_dir, "het_F_distribution_combined.png"), combined_plot,
#       width = 10, height = 5, dpi = 300, bg = "white")

QC 6: Post-GenotypeQCtoHRC checks

Benchmarking

  • Why is the GenotypeQCtoHRC benchmark file missing?

Table

Plot

runtime_plt <- ggplot(summary, aes(x = reorder(rule, mean_runtime_min), 
                                 y = mean_runtime_min)) +
  geom_col(fill = "steelblue") +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = mean_runtime_min - ifelse(is.na(sd_runtime_min), 0, sd_runtime_min),
                    ymax = mean_runtime_min + ifelse(is.na(sd_runtime_min), 0, sd_runtime_min)),
                width = 0.3, colour = "black", linewidth = 0.6) +
  coord_flip() +
  labs(x = "Rule", y = "Runtime (minutes)", 
       title = "Mean runtime per rule (± SD)") +
  theme_classic(base_size = 16) +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold", size = 18),
    axis.text = element_text(size = 14),
    axis.title = element_text(size = 16),
    panel.grid.major.y = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank()
  )



mem_plt <- ggplot(summary, aes(x = reorder(rule, mean_ram_gb), 
                                y = mean_ram_gb)) +
  geom_col(fill = "darkorange") +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = mean_ram_gb - ifelse(is.na(sd_ram_gb), 0, sd_ram_gb),
                    ymax = mean_ram_gb + ifelse(is.na(sd_ram_gb), 0, sd_ram_gb)),
                width = 0.3, colour = "black", linewidth = 0.6) +
  coord_flip() +
  labs(x = "Rule", y = "RAM (GB)", 
       title = "Mean RAM usage per rule (± SD)") +
  theme_classic(base_size = 16) +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold", size = 18),
    axis.text = element_text(size = 14),
    axis.title = element_text(size = 16),
    panel.grid.major.y = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank()
  )

plot_grid(runtime_plt, mem_plt)
